title: Agave neocernua
intro: Agave neocernua, also known as Agave cerulata, is a species of succulent plant native to Mexico, belonging to the Agave genus. The health benefits of Agave neocernua include its potential anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which may aid in wound healing and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Agave neocernua is commonly used in herbal preparations such as tinctures, infusions, and ointments, which are traditionally used to treat various skin conditions, including eczema and acne.
